title: RPYR-0005 — `mmap.move(dest, src, count)` aborts on unclamped `size - dest` underflow → OOB slice (`mmap.rs:903`)
found_by: git-history-analyzer (similar-bug; the scanner has no pattern for this shape)
cpython_behavior: raises ValueError for an out-of-range move
guarded_twin: crates/stdlib/src/mmap.rs — write() (L1150) checks `pos > size || size - pos < data.len()` FIRST; flush()/FlushOptions uses checked_sub; move_ uses the raw subtraction
notes: SCANNER-BLIND, HISTORY-FOUND: move() is a #[pymethod] (py-tier), but it carries no .unwrap()/.expect()/panic!/.args[ token on the offending line — the panic is a raw `size - dest` unsigned subtraction and then an OOB slice write `new_args.args[0]`-style index — so the pattern scanner cannot see it. The history agent's similar-bug detection caught it by pattern-matching the guard idiom (write() has the guard, move_ does not). Debug builds panic at the `size - dest` subtraction (overflow-checks); release builds wrap past the guard, then the OOB slice at mmap.rs:903 aborts.
